acceptance_results.criterion: Migration drops all legacy campaigns(slug) foreign keys before dropping campaigns_slug_key.
Migration does not use DROP CONSTRAINT CASCADE.

completed_work: Replaced a hard-coded acquisition_transactions FK drop with catalog-driven removal of every FK that references public.campaigns(slug).
Kept the migration explicit and bounded by avoiding CASCADE.
Added a repository migration guard test covering FK discovery, DDL ordering, no CASCADE, and tenant acquisition indexes.
Recorded TMP-049 issue, work order, slice manifest entry, domain brief, story, and value-gate evidence.
known_gaps: The fix was verified with static migration tests and repository tests; it was not run against the operator's remote PostgreSQL database to avoid mutating live data from the agent session.
operator_message: TMP-049 removes the migration startup blocker reported by acquisition-api without changing runtime business logic.
risk_notes: Dropping legacy slug foreign keys is intentional for tenant-scoped duplicate campaign slugs. Any consumers that still require referential integrity on campaign_slug need a future tenant-aware composite FK design.
summary: Fixed acquisition-api startup schema bootstrap by removing all legacy foreign keys that reference public.campaigns(slug) before dropping campaigns_slug_key.

validation.output_excerpt: TMP-049 assignment_decision allow; no blocking reasons
